content.problemStatement:
You are given NNN identical squares, each with side length AAA. All the squares have their sides parallel to the x−axisx-axisx−axis and y−axisy-axisy−axis. That is, the squares are not tilted. You have to take several (possibly, zero or all) squares and rearrange them to obtain a mega square. The mega square can't have any gap in the enclosed region or have overlapping squares. Also, you cannot rotate any square.

Output the side length of the largest mega square that you can obtain.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains two space-separated integers N,AN, AN,A.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing one integer - the side length of the largest square you can obtain.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers N and A, where N is the number of identical squares of side length A.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing one integer – the maximum possible side length of the mega square.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
For each test case we take s = floor(sqrt(N)) and output s*A.
Test1: floor(sqrt(3))=1 → 1*2=2.
Test2: floor(sqrt(5))=2 → 2*3=6.
Test3: floor(sqrt(16))=4 → 4*18=72.
Test4: floor(sqrt(11))=3 → 3*8=24.
Test5: floor(sqrt(8))=2 → 2*6=12.

solution.approach: To build a mega square we must arrange the chosen squares in a grid of s rows and s columns, using exactly s^2 squares. The largest s such that s^2 ≤ N is s = floor(sqrt(N)). The side length of the resulting mega square is s * A. For each test case we compute s with an integer square‑root routine and output s*A.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – each test case needs only O(1) arithmetic operations.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are stored.

code.Python3:
import sys, math

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it))
        A = int(next(it))
        s = math.isqrt(N)
        out.append(str(s * A))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
